description: Replacement for the Indian Penal Code effective from July 1, 2024. New criminal code of India.

sections.title: Punishment for Murder (replaces IPC 302)
sections.chapter: VI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Whoever commits murder shall be punished with death, or imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.simplified: Murder punishment remains the same as IPC 302: death penalty or life imprisonment plus fine. This section replaces IPC Section 302.
sections.punishment: Death or life imprisonment and fine

sections.title: Punishment for Rape (replaces IPC 376)
sections.chapter: VI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Whoever commits rape shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than ten years, but which may extend to imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.simplified: Rape punishment under BNS: minimum 10 years rigorous imprisonment, can extend to life. Gang rape: minimum 20 years to life. This replaces the old IPC Section 376.
sections.punishment: Minimum 10 years RI to life imprisonment

sections.title: Rape of minor (replaces IPC 376AB)
sections.chapter: VI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Whoever commits rape on a woman when she is under twelve years of age shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than twenty years, but which may extend to imprisonment for life, which shall mean imprisonment for the remainder of that person's natural life, and with fine, or with death.
sections.simplified: Rape of a girl under 12 years carries a minimum of 20 years imprisonment, up to life imprisonment (natural life) or even the death penalty under the new law.

sections.title: Sexual Harassment (replaces IPC 354A)
sections.chapter: VI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: A man committing any of the following acts: physical contact and advances involving unwelcome and explicit sexual overtures; a demand or request for sexual favours; showing pornography against the will of a woman; making sexually coloured remarks, shall be guilty of the offence of sexual harassment.
sections.simplified: Sexual harassment under BNS covers: unwanted physical contact, demanding sexual favours, forcing someone to watch porn, making sexual remarks. Punishment: up to 3 years and/or fine.

sections.title: Assault on Woman with Intent to Outrage her Modesty (replaces IPC 354)
sections.chapter: VI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any woman, intending to outrage or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby outrage her modesty, shall be punished with imprisonment for not less than one year but which may extend to five years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.simplified: Assaulting a woman to outrage her modesty: 1-5 years imprisonment and fine. Same as old IPC 354 but now under BNS Section 74.

sections.title: Stalking (replaces IPC 354D)
sections.chapter: VI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Any man who follows a woman and contacts, or attempts to contact such woman to foster personal interaction repeatedly despite a clear indication of disinterest by such woman, or monitors the use by a woman of the internet, email or any other form of electronic communication, commits the offence of stalking.
sections.simplified: Stalking under BNS: following someone, repeated unwanted contact, or monitoring online activity. First offence: up to 3 years. Second offence: up to 5 years. Same as earlier but now under BNS.
sections.punishment: First: up to 3 years. Repeat: up to 5 years

sections.title: Attempt to Murder (replaces IPC 307)
sections.chapter: VI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Whoever does any act with such intention or knowledge, and under such circumstances that, if he by that act caused death, he would be guilty of murder, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.simplified: Attempted murder under BNS: up to 10 years. If hurt is caused: up to life imprisonment. This replaces IPC Section 307.
sections.punishment: Up to 10 years or life imprisonment

sections.title: Voluntarily Causing Hurt (replaces IPC 323)
sections.chapter: VI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Whoever voluntarily causes hurt shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees, or with both.
sections.simplified: Voluntarily hurting someone: up to 1 year jail and/or ₹10,000 fine. Note the fine has increased from ₹1,000 (IPC) to ₹10,000 (BNS).

sections.title: Voluntarily Causing Grievous Hurt (replaces IPC 325)
sections.chapter: VI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Whoever voluntarily causes grievous hurt shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.simplified: Causing serious injuries (fractures, disfigurement, etc.) carries up to 7 years imprisonment and fine. Same as IPC 325 but under BNS.

sections.title: Theft (replaces IPC 378-379)
sections.text: Whoever commits theft shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
sections.simplified: Theft under BNS: up to 3 years jail and/or fine. Same punishment as IPC 379 but now combined definition and punishment.

sections.title: Criminal Breach of Trust (replaces IPC 405-406)
sections.text: Whoever commits criminal breach of trust shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
sections.simplified: Misusing property or money entrusted to you: up to 3 years and/or fine. Public servants or agents face up to 7 years.

sections.title: Cheating (replaces IPC 415/420)
sections.text: Whoever cheats and thereby dishonestly induces the person deceived to deliver any property to any person shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.simplified: Cheating by inducing someone to hand over property: up to 7 years and fine. This replaces the famous IPC 420.

sections.title: Criminal Intimidation (replaces IPC 503/506)
sections.text: Whoever threatens another with any injury to his person, reputation or property with intent to cause alarm to that person commits criminal intimidation.
sections.simplified: Threatening someone is criminal intimidation: up to 2 years. Death threats: up to 7 years. Now under BNS Section 351 (replaces IPC 503/506).

sections.title: Defamation (replaces IPC 499/500)
sections.text: Whoever by words either spoken or intended to be read or by signs or by visible representations makes or publishes any imputation concerning any person intending to harm the reputation of such person is said to defame that person.
sections.simplified: Defamation under BNS: same definition as IPC 499/500. Publishing false statements damaging someone's reputation: up to 2 years and/or fine.

sections.title: Cruelty by Husband or Relatives (replaces IPC 498A)
sections.chapter: VI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Whoever, being the husband or the relative of the husband of a woman, subjects such woman to cruelty shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to three years and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.simplified: Dowry harassment/domestic cruelty under BNS: same as IPC 498A. Up to 3 years jail and fine. Cognizable and non-bailable.

sections.title: Organised Crime (NEW)
sections.chapter: VII - Of Organised Crime and Terrorism
sections.text: Whoever commits or attempts to commit or abets or participates in an act of organised crime shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine which shall not be less than five lakh rupees.
sections.simplified: NEW in BNS: Organized crime is now separately defined and carries life imprisonment with minimum ₹5 lakh fine. This covers syndicates, gangs, and organized criminal groups.
sections.punishment: Up to life imprisonment and minimum ₹5 lakh fine

sections.title: Terrorism (NEW)
sections.chapter: VII - Of Organised Crime and Terrorism
sections.text: Whoever commits or attempts to commit a terrorist act, or aids, abets, counsels or procures the commission of such act, or conspires, facilitates or prepares for such act, shall be punished.
sections.simplified: NEW in BNS: Terrorism is now specifically defined in the criminal code. Punishment ranges from minimum 5 years to death penalty depending on the severity.

sections.title: Snatching (NEW)
sections.text: Whoever commits theft with the intent to commit theft, suddenly or quickly or forcibly seizes or secures or grabs or takes away from any person, or from his possession any moveable property, commits snatching.
sections.simplified: NEW in BNS: Snatching (chain-snatching, phone-snatching, etc.) is now a separate crime punishable with up to 3 years jail. This was not separately defined in the old IPC.

sections.title: IPC to BNS Section Mapping Guide
sections.text: Key mappings: IPC 302 → BNS 103, IPC 304 → BNS 105, IPC 307 → BNS 109, IPC 354 → BNS 74, IPC 354A → BNS 75, IPC 375/376 → BNS 63/64, IPC 378/379 → BNS 303, IPC 420 → BNS 318, IPC 498A → BNS 85, IPC 499/500 → BNS 356, IPC 503/506 → BNS 351.
sections.simplified: The BNS replaces the IPC from July 1, 2024. Key section mappings: Murder (302→103), Rape (376→64), Theft (379→303), Cheating (420→318), Dowry Cruelty (498A→85). Both old and new section numbers are commonly used during the transition period.
